speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Hear you, Master Steward, where’s our master?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Are we undone, cast off, nothing remaining?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Alack, my fellows, what should I say to you?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Let me be recorded by the righteous gods,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I am as poor as you.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Such a house broke?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: So noble a master fall’n, all gone, and not

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: One friend to take his fortune by the arm

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: And go along with him?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: As we do turn our backs

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: From our companion thrown into his grave,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: So his familiars to his buried fortunes

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Slink all away, leave their false vows with him,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Like empty purses picked; and his poor self,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: A dedicated beggar to the air,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: With his disease of all-shunned poverty,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Walks, like contempt, alone.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: More of our fellows.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: All broken implements of a ruined house.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Yet do our hearts wear Timon’s livery.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: That see I by our faces. We are fellows still,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Serving alike in sorrow. Leaked is our bark,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: And we, poor mates, stand on the dying deck,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Hearing the surges threat. We must all part

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Into this sea of air.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Good fellows all,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: The latest of my wealth I’ll share amongst you.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Wherever we shall meet, for Timon’s sake

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Let’s yet be fellows. Let’s shake our heads and say,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: As ’twere a knell unto our master’s fortunes,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: “We have seen better days.”

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Let each take some.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Nay, put out all your hands. Not one word more.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Thus part we rich in sorrow, parting poor.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O, the fierce wretchedness that glory brings us!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Who would not wish to be from wealth exempt,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Since riches point to misery and contempt?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Who would be so mocked with glory, or to live

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: But in a dream of friendship,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To have his pomp and all what state compounds

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: But only painted, like his varnished friends?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Poor honest lord, brought low by his own heart,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Undone by goodness! Strange unusual blood

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: When man’s worst sin is he does too much good!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Who then dares to be half so kind again?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For bounty, that makes gods, do still mar men.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: My dearest lord, blest to be most accursed,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Rich only to be wretched, thy great fortunes

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Are made thy chief afflictions. Alas, kind lord!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: He’s flung in rage from this ingrateful seat

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Of monstrous friends,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Nor has he with him to supply his life,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Or that which can command it.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I’ll follow and inquire him out.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I’ll ever serve his mind with my best will.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Whilst I have gold, I’ll be his steward still.
